tasting_rooms.name: Champagne Taittinger Cellars
tasting_rooms.pricing: Guided cellar tour with tasting from EUR 40; Comtes de Champagne prestige tasting from EUR 95
tasting_rooms.description: Hour-long guided tours of Taittinger's UNESCO-listed Gallo-Roman chalk crayeres beneath Reims, closing with a tasting of Brut Reserve or Comtes de Champagne.

tasting_rooms.name: Veuve Clicquot Visit Center
tasting_rooms.pricing: Classic cellar visit with Yellow Label tasting from EUR 45; Prestige vintage experiences higher
tasting_rooms.description: The Veuve Clicquot visit center on the Butte Saint-Nicaise leads through the chalk cellars to a guided tasting of Yellow Label, the blended rose and vintage flights.

tasting_rooms.name: Moet & Chandon Cellars
tasting_rooms.pricing: Imperial tour with seated tasting from EUR 48; Grand Vintage and private experiences to EUR 235
tasting_rooms.description: Moet & Chandon opens its 28 kilometres of UNESCO-listed cellars on the Avenue de Champagne, with guided tours and a seated tasting of Imperial Brut or Grand Vintage.

tasting_rooms.name: Champagne Mercier
tasting_rooms.pricing: Train cellar tour with one-glass tasting from around EUR 20; premium tastings higher
tasting_rooms.description: Mercier runs the only little-train tour through its 18 kilometres of Epernay cellars, with a panoramic lift, the train circuit and a Mercier brut tasting.

tasting_rooms.name: Maison Mumm
tasting_rooms.address: 31 rue du Champ de Mars, 51100 Reims, France
tasting_rooms.pricing: Grand Cru cellar tour with Cordon Rouge tasting from EUR 30; vintage experiences higher
tasting_rooms.description: G.H. Mumm guides visitors through almost 25 kilometres of cellars in central Reims, finishing with a tasting of the Cordon Rouge flagship brut and vintages.

verified.address_quoted: 31 Rue du Champ de Mars, 51100 - Reims

tasting_rooms.name: Maison Ruinart
tasting_rooms.pricing: Crayeres guided visit with tasting from EUR 50; Blanc de Blancs and Dom Ruinart experiences higher
tasting_rooms.description: The oldest Champagne house opens its 8 kilometres of UNESCO-listed chalk crayeres 40 metres beneath Reims, centred on the Blanc de Blancs and Dom Ruinart.

tasting_rooms.name: Domaine Pommery
tasting_rooms.address: 5 Place du General Gouraud, 51100 Reims, France
tasting_rooms.pricing: Self-guided or guided cellar visit with tasting from around EUR 25; masterclasses higher
tasting_rooms.description: Pommery shows its Elizabethan estate and 18 kilometres of art-lined Gallo-Roman chalk pits 30 metres underground, with tastings of Apanage Brut and the Cuvee Louise.

tasting_rooms.name: C. Comme Champagne
tasting_rooms.pricing: Self-serve tasting flights by the glass from around EUR 5; producer flights higher with charcuterie boards
tasting_rooms.description: A grower-champagne tasting shop and bar in central Epernay representing dozens of small producers, pouring walk-in flights by the glass with cheese and charcuterie.

tasting_rooms.name: 520 Champagnes et Vins d'Auteurs
tasting_rooms.pricing: By-the-glass tasting flights vary; free Saturday-morning producer tastings 10:30 to 13:00
tasting_rooms.description: Run by former champagne ambassador Pierre-Yves Cainjo, 520 carries some 650 references from 180 growers and houses, with free Saturday producer tastings.

tasting_rooms.name: Tresors de Champagne, La Boutique
tasting_rooms.pricing: Weekly rotating tasting selection by the glass; private tastings on request
tasting_rooms.description: The Club Tresors de Champagne boutique gathers more than 170 Special Club cuvees from 28 grower members, with a weekly selection open for tasting by the glass.
